entries.name: basic_auth
entries.summary: Match HTTP Basic Authorization on the inbound request
description: Match HTTP Basic Authorization on the inbound request
Decoded credentials must match the username and bcrypt password hash configured in the rule

args.name: password
args.description: bcrypt cost hash corresponding to that user

entries.name: cookie
entries.summary: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: default
entries.summary: Select the default (fallback) rule
description: Select the default (fallback) rule

entries.name: form
entries.summary: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: header
entries.summary: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: host
entries.summary: Supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: method
entries.summary: Match inbound HTTP method (verb)
description: Match inbound HTTP method (verb)

args.name: method
args.description: canonical method name such as GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, CONNECT, OPTIONS, TRACE

entries.name: path
entries.summary: Supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: postform
entries.summary: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: proto
entries.summary: Match inbound scheme or protocol family
description: Match inbound scheme or protocol family
http/https match cleartext vs TLS regardless of HTTP version
h1 matches cleartext HTTP/1.x; h2 matches TLS HTTP/2; h2c matches cleartext HTTP/2; h3 matches TLS HTTP/3

args.name: proto
args.description: http, https, h1 (cleartext HTTP/1.x), h2 (TLS HTTP/2), h2c (cleartext HTTP/2), or h3 (TLS HTTP/3)

entries.name: query
entries.summary: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: remote
entries.summary: Match remote client IP against an address or CIDR
description: Match remote client IP against an address or CIDR
Bare IPv4 input is treated as /32 for an exact host match
Use /128 for an exact IPv6 host; bare IPv6 input expands to /32, and wider prefixes match the whole subnet

args.name: ip|cidr
args.description: IPv4/IPv6 CIDR, or a bare IPv4 address for /32 exact match

entries.name: resp_header
entries.summary: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Value supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: route
entries.summary: Supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern
description: Supports string, glob pattern, or regex pattern

entries.name: status
entries.summary: Match current post-phase response status (exact, range, or class)
description: Match current post-phase response status (exact, range, or class)
Evaluated after upstream responds; earlier post-phase rewrites can change the status seen here

entries.name: add
entries.summary: Add a value to a field in the request or response
description: Add a value to a field in the request or response

args.name: target
args.description: the target to add, can be

entries.name: error
entries.summary: Send an HTTP error response and terminate processing
description: Send an HTTP error response and terminate processing

args.name: code
args.description: the http status code to return

entries.name: handle
entries.summary: Serve the request with a registered in-process HTTP handler
description: Serve the request with a registered in-process HTTP handler

entries.name: log
entries.summary: Write a log line from a template
description: Write a log line from a template
The template supports the following variables:
Request: the request object
Response: the response object
Example:

args.name: path
args.description: the log path (/dev/stdout for stdout, /dev/stderr for stderr)

entries.name: notify
entries.summary: Send a notification from templates
description: Send a notification from templates
The template supports the following variables:
Request: the request object
Response: the response object
Example:

args.name: provider
args.description: the notification provider (must be defined in config `providers.notification`)

entries.name: proxy
entries.summary: Proxy the request to the specified absolute URL
description: Proxy the request to the specified absolute URL

args.name: to
args.description: the url to proxy to, must be an absolute URL

entries.name: redirect
entries.summary: Redirect request to another URL
description: Redirect request to another URL

args.name: to
args.description: the url to redirect to, can be relative or absolute URL

entries.name: remove
entries.summary: Remove a field from the request or response
description: Remove a field from the request or response

args.name: target
args.description: the target to remove, can be

entries.name: require_auth
entries.summary: Require HTTP authentication for incoming requests
description: Require HTTP authentication for incoming requests

entries.name: require_basic_auth
entries.summary: Require HTTP basic authentication for incoming requests
description: Require HTTP basic authentication for incoming requests

entries.name: rewrite
entries.summary: Rewrite a request path from one prefix to another
description: Rewrite a request path from one prefix to another

args.name: from
args.description: the path to rewrite, must start with /

args.name: to
args.description: the path to rewrite to, must start with /

entries.name: route
entries.summary: Route the request to another route
description: Route the request to another route

entries.name: serve
entries.summary: Serve static files from a local file system path
description: Serve static files from a local file system path

args.name: root
args.description: the file system path to serve, must be an existing directory

entries.name: set
entries.summary: Set a field in the request or response
description: Set a field in the request or response

args.name: target
args.description: the target to set, can be

entries.name: upstream
entries.summary: Pass the request to the upstream
description: Pass the request to the upstream

entries.name: body
entries.summary: Override the request body that will be sent to the upstream
description: Override the request body that will be sent to the upstream
The template supports the following variables:
Request: the request object
Example:

entries.name: cookie
entries.summary: Rewrite cookies attached to the forwarded request
description: Rewrite cookies attached to the forwarded request
Set updates an existing cookie or creates one with that name
Add appends another Cookie entry carrying the same or a new name

args.name: value
args.description: template evaluated to the cookie value forwarded upstream

entries.name: header
entries.summary: Rewrite request headers forwarded upstream
description: Rewrite request headers forwarded upstream
Set replaces all values for a name; add appends another value; remove deletes the header

args.name: value
args.description: template evaluated to produce new header values

entries.name: query
entries.summary: Rewrite query parameters on the forwarded request
description: Rewrite query parameters on the forwarded request
Uses Set semantics for overwrite and Add semantics to append duplicates

args.name: value
args.description: template evaluated to the parameter value passed upstream

entries.name: resp_body
entries.summary: Override the response body that will be sent to the client
description: Override the response body that will be sent to the client
The template supports the following variables:
Request: the request object
Response: the response object
Example:

entries.name: resp_header
entries.summary: Rewrite response headers after upstream returns
description: Rewrite response headers after upstream returns
Applied in post phase to the outbound response envelope

args.name: value
args.description: template evaluated to produce header values returned to clients

entries.name: status
entries.summary: Override the status code that will be sent to the client
description: Override the status code that will be sent to the client

entries.name: $arg
entries.summary: Query parameter value lookup
description: Query parameter value lookup
Reads the current request query parameters, including earlier rule mutations

args.name: [index]
args.description: Optional zero-based value index; defaults to 0

entries.name: $cookie
entries.summary: Request cookie value lookup
description: Request cookie value lookup
Reads parsed cookies from the current forwarded request state

args.name: [index]
args.description: Optional zero-based value index; defaults to 0

entries.name: $form
entries.summary: Parsed form value lookup with query fallback
description: Parsed form value lookup with query fallback
Reads req.Form after ParseForm, so body form values and query parameters are both visible here

args.name: [index]
args.description: Optional zero-based value index; defaults to 0

entries.name: $header
entries.summary: Request header value lookup
description: Request header value lookup
Uses the exact header key provided; header names are not canonicalized during lookup

args.name: [index]
args.description: Optional zero-based value index; defaults to 0

entries.name: $postform
entries.summary: Parsed request-body form value lookup
description: Parsed request-body form value lookup
Reads req.PostForm from body form data only; query parameters are excluded

args.name: [index]
args.description: Optional zero-based value index; defaults to 0

entries.name: $redacted
entries.summary: Mask a sensitive value for logs or notifications
description: Mask a sensitive value for logs or notifications
Keeps only the edges of the input visible and replaces the middle with asterisks

args.name: value
args.description: Literal text or the output of another variable expression

entries.name: $remote_addr
entries.summary: Raw remote client address
description: Raw remote client address
Usually the host:port string from the incoming request's RemoteAddr field

entries.name: $remote_host
entries.summary: Remote client IP from RemoteAddr
description: Remote client IP from RemoteAddr
Split from the request remote address. Empty when the address cannot be parsed into host and port

entries.name: $remote_port
entries.summary: Remote client port from RemoteAddr
description: Remote client port from RemoteAddr
Split from the request remote address. Empty when the address cannot be parsed into host and port

entries.name: $req_addr
entries.summary: Inbound request host as sent by the client
description: Inbound request host as sent by the client
Includes the port when one is present in the Host or authority component

entries.name: $req_content_length
entries.summary: Inbound request content length
description: Inbound request content length
String form of the request body content length. May be -1 when unknown

entries.name: $req_content_type
entries.summary: Inbound request Content-Type header
description: Inbound request Content-Type header
Reads the current Content-Type header value from the request

entries.name: $req_host
entries.summary: Inbound request host without port
description: Inbound request host without port
Derived from the Host or authority component after stripping any explicit port

entries.name: $req_method
entries.summary: Inbound HTTP method (verb)
description: Inbound HTTP method (verb)
Reads the request method exactly as the current handler sees it

entries.name: $req_path
entries.summary: Inbound request path
description: Inbound request path
Uses the URL path that rule matching and upstream forwarding operate on

entries.name: $req_port
entries.summary: Inbound request port
description: Inbound request port
Split from the Host or authority component; empty when no explicit port was sent

entries.name: $req_query
entries.summary: Raw inbound query string
description: Raw inbound query string
Returns the query portion without the leading ?

entries.name: $req_scheme
entries.summary: Inbound request scheme
description: Inbound request scheme
Returns https when TLS is present on the request; otherwise returns http

entries.name: $req_uri
entries.summary: Request URI path plus query
description: Request URI path plus query
Equivalent to the path and query string together, without any fragment

entries.name: $req_url
entries.summary: Full inbound request URL
description: Full inbound request URL
Includes scheme, host, path, and query when that information is available on the request URL

entries.name: $resp_content_length
entries.summary: Current response body length
description: Current response body length
Uses the buffered body size after response rewrites, otherwise the original or header Content-Length value

entries.name: $resp_content_type
entries.summary: Current response Content-Type header
description: Current response Content-Type header
Available in post phase after upstream response headers and any earlier response mutations

entries.name: $resp_header
entries.summary: Response header value lookup
description: Response header value lookup
Reads the current response header map in post phase using the exact key provided

args.name: [index]
args.description: Optional zero-based value index; defaults to 0

entries.name: $status_code
entries.summary: Current response status code
description: Current response status code
Available in post phase after upstream responds. Earlier post-phase rewrites can change the value seen here

entries.name: $upstream_addr
entries.summary: Selected upstream host and port
description: Selected upstream host and port
Combines the chosen upstream host and port as a single address string

entries.name: $upstream_host
entries.summary: Selected upstream host
description: Selected upstream host
The host portion of the upstream target chosen for this request

entries.name: $upstream_name
entries.summary: Selected upstream route name
description: Selected upstream route name
Comes from route context after routing has selected the upstream target

entries.name: $upstream_port
entries.summary: Selected upstream port
description: Selected upstream port
The port portion of the upstream target chosen for this request

entries.name: $upstream_scheme
entries.summary: Selected upstream scheme
description: Selected upstream scheme
Examples include http, https, or h2c depending on the upstream target

entries.name: $upstream_url
entries.summary: Selected upstream URL
description: Selected upstream URL
Full upstream target URL resolved for the current request

sections.title: Control Flow
sections.description: Conditional branches inside a rule do-block (inherits the parent rule's phase).

entries.name: default
entries.summary: Select the default (fallback) rule
description: Select the default (fallback) rule

entries.name: if
entries.summary: Inline conditional block inside a do-body
description: Semantics:
- Evaluated in the same phase the parent rule runs
- If <on-expr> matches, run the nested commands in-order
- Otherwise do nothing
NOTE: Per current design decision, we keep this permissive:
nested blocks may use response matchers and response commands; no extra phase validation is performed

entries.name: elif
entries.summary: Chained conditional block inside a do-body
description: NOTE: `elif`/`else` must appear on the same line as the preceding closing brace (`}`)
e.g. `} elif ... {` and `} else {`

entries.name: else
entries.summary: Chained conditional block inside a do-body
description: NOTE: `elif`/`else` must appear on the same line as the preceding closing brace (`}`)
e.g. `} elif ... {` and `} else {`
